hero.sub: Takeover is a full-stack web app framework — SSR, HMR, bundling, i18n — built on Node built-ins and the Web Components standard. No Vite. No Webpack. No node_modules.

counter.title: Reactive store
counter.sub: EventTarget under the hood. Per-key subscriptions.
counter.label: count

quickstart.title: Up and running in three commands.
quickstart.sub: Node 18 or higher. That is the only requirement. Really.

demos.title: Real patterns. Working code paths.
demos.sub: Each demo mirrors a real API from the framework. Authentication, form binding with validation, route guards, async fetch with loading states and custom events. Click through each one.

performance.title: Audited. Not assumed.
performance.sub: Zero dependencies means zero bytes you didn't ask for. SSR at the edge ships a parsed page in one hop. The numbers are what they say they are.

features.title: Everything you need. Nothing you don't.
features.sub: A complete frontend stack assembled from the platform. Each piece is small, readable, and replaceable.

structure.title: File-system routing. Predictable layout.
structure.sub: Pages live in app/. Shared pieces live in components/. The framework lives in core/. That is the whole story.

arch.title: How a request flows.
arch.sub: No virtual DOM. No hydration mismatch. The browser parses Declarative Shadow DOM before JS runs — hydration is just event binding.

cta.title: Take over your stack.
cta.sub: Clone the starter, ship in an afternoon.

footer.built: Built with Node, the platform, and not much else.
